title: dichanthelium clandestinum
intro: Dichanthelium clandestinum, also known as hidden rosinweed or hidden panic grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones. Drinking teas or infusions made from the plant can provide health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Dichanthelium clandestinum can be made by drying the leaves and stems of the plant and then using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
